title: Repair remote executor /api/v1/results persistence and prove recovery
objective: Repair the remote executor result-persistence chain so terminal task results are durably visible through /api/v1/results. Treat this as infrastructure work, not workflow work. Finish with proof: a cheap smoke task must yield a terminal result in /api/v1/results, and then one provisioning-class probe must also yield a terminal result.

references.description: Most recent proof that smoke task itself failed to produce a terminal result, implying remote executor result persistence is broken.
Current branch A blocker showing provisioning-class tasks are accepted/running but never land terminal results.
Main-thread summary of the current remote provisioning blocker.

required_work.description: Read fresh remote /api/v1/state and /api/v1/results facts. Confirm the failure mode with new evidence before patching: tasks are accepted/running but terminal results are not durably returned by /api/v1/results.

Locate the exact result-persistence path in the remote controller/executor stack. Identify where terminal task outcomes are supposed to be written, indexed, or exposed to /api/v1/results, and determine why the write/visibility step is failing.

Implement the smallest correct fix in the controller/executor infrastructure, deploy it to the remote environment, and document exactly what changed. This is an infrastructure repair task; do not mix in workflow feature changes.
Run one cheap smoke task whose only purpose is to produce a strict terminal JSON result. Confirm that the task reaches terminal state and that the result becomes visible through /api/v1/results.

After the smoke task passes, run exactly one provisioning-class probe, preferably the single-child onboard probe already used in branch A. Confirm that it also produces a terminal result in /api/v1/results.

Write detailed logs and a final structured result. If both smoke and provisioning probe now persist results, explicitly mark branch A blocker as unblocked at infrastructure level. If not, stop at the first new blocker with evidence.

do_not: Do not continue the full 5-agent workflow deployment before /api/v1/results persistence is proven fixed.
Do not treat this as a community workflow bug unless the remote result chain is first proven healthy.
Do not stop at shallow polling or another narration-only status update.
Do not keep redispatching expensive or repeated provisioning tasks without first proving result persistence with a cheap smoke task.
Do not mix UI work, protocol redesign, or unrelated feature work into this task.
acceptance: A root-cause analysis of the /api/v1/results persistence failure is captured.
A concrete infrastructure fix is implemented and deployed, or the first hard blocker to deployment is captured with evidence.
At least one smoke task yields a terminal result that is visible through /api/v1/results.
At least one provisioning-class probe yields a terminal result that is visible through /api/v1/results.
The final structured result states clearly whether the branch A remote provisioning blocker is now unblocked at infrastructure level.

deliverables.description: Evidence folder for reproduction, root cause, patch/deploy, smoke verification, provisioning-probe verification, and final closeout.
Main-thread log root for the detailed execution log.

working_principles: This is infrastructure repair first. Business workflow deployment remains downstream of result persistence.
Evidence beats narration. Fresh remote state/results evidence must be captured before and after the fix.
Use the cheapest decisive verification first: smoke task, then one provisioning probe.
If the fix cannot be deployed, stop at the first real blocker and state it precisely.
